data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m` for `"express"`.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to set up hot reload with `dotnet watch test` so they can iterate on the failing tests. I can see one test will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m for "express".

To set up hot reload, I need to:
1. Add the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` RunSettingsArguments for hot reload or just use `dotnet watch test`
2. Start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-04dee4d209114488b2bd5cdf093fd17a && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}
